t_ch: …不，芙洛拉，我不会走的。
t_en: ...No. Flora, I will not go.

t_ch: 就在今晚，我要和一个女人道别。她在我的人生路上扮演了无可替代的重要角色——尽管她对此并不自知。
t_en: Tonight, I will bid farewell to a woman. This woman has played an irreplaceable role in my life... Even if she does not know it.

t_ch: 善良…细腻…隐忍…她身上的美好品质我细数不尽。有她陪伴的这段时光，我的人生过得如此顺利…她为我扛下了沉重的冗务，让我能够专心于自己的事业。
t_en: Kind... Meticulous... Patient... She has more good qualities than I could ever list. With her by my side, my life could not have been smoother... She took on the weight of my responsibilities so that I could continue to focus on my own career.

t_ch: 除此之外，还有一件更重要的事。假如我没有折返回来，假如这场对话没有发生——她的人生会坠入谷底，我会亲手制造出她的不幸。
t_en: But there is something more important than all this. If I had not turned back, if I had never had this conversation... Her life would fall to the depths and I would personally have caused her unhappiness.

t_ch: 我绝对、绝对无法饶恕做出那种选择的自己。所以，芙洛拉——在你解开那个束缚自己的心结之前，我是绝对不会离开的。
t_en: I would never forgive the version of myself that would do this. So, Flora... I will not leave until you unravel the knot that is consuming you from within.

t_ch: 傻瓜，莱斯利…你这傻瓜……
t_en: Lesley, you idiot... You really are...

t_ch: 我一直都…一直都喜欢……
t_en: I always... I always loved...

t_ch: 不…不对。
t_en: No... That's not right.

t_ch: <size=36>我一直都爱着你啊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>I will always love you!</size>

t_ch: 我一度把她的付出当作理所当然，直到很久之后才意识到自己有多幼稚和愚蠢。对此，我恳请她的原谅……
t_en: I took everything that she did for me for granted. Only after all this time did I realize how childish and idiotic I was. I hope that she can forgive me for this...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我有一个发现。
t_en: {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, I have realized something.

t_ch: 可以和您分享吗？
t_en: May I share it with you?

t_ch: 我在听。
t_en: I'm listening.

t_ch: 就在刚才，有那么一个瞬间…我突然觉得，之前一直困扰着我的问题似乎没那么重要了。
t_en: There was a moment just now... I suddenly felt like all of the problems that had been bothering me before were no longer important.

t_ch: 什么问题？
t_en: Yes?

t_ch: 「我是谁」——自这具形体诞生之初，同样的问题就一直萦绕着我。
t_en: "Who am I"... This question has been following me since the moment I first took this form.

t_ch: 是芙洛拉帮你看清的吗？
t_en: Was it Flora that helped you realize this?

t_ch: 我想是的。
t_en: I think so.

t_ch: 「我是谁」——自这具形体诞生之初，同样的问题就一直萦绕着我。
t_en: "Who am I"... This question has been following me since the moment I first took this form.

t_ch: 在阅读芙洛拉的记忆的时候，我第一次有了身处画中的感觉。她的话语，她的情绪，都让我的心实实在在地为之颤动。
t_en: When I was reading Flora's memories, I felt like I was inside a painting for the first time. Her words and emotions truly shook my heart.

t_ch: 我在那一刻终于确信：我无需再怀疑自己的存在。我就是莱斯利•迪恩生命的延续。
t_en: At that moment, I finally believed: I no longer need to doubt my own existence. I am an extension of the life of Lesley Dean.

t_ch: 构成我的是莱斯利•迪恩本人的记忆，辅以人们对他的印象和追思。我开始觉得…只这两点已经足够使人为人，使我为我——我不再需要一副空洞的躯壳作为佐证。
t_en: I am composed of Lesley Dean's memories, along with people's ideas and impressions of him. I have started to feel... That these two things are enough to form a person, to make me, me... I no longer need this hollow body to prove my existence.

t_ch: 谢谢您，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}。少了您的帮助，只凭我一个人做不到任何这些。
t_en: Thank you, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. Without your help, I would never have been able to do any of this alone.

t_ch: 我们离开这里吧。我感觉…有些累了。
t_en: Let us leave this place. I'm feeling... tired.

t_ch: 我们回来了，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}……
t_en: We're back, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 莱斯利的声音变得虚弱了，他的皮肤和衣物渐渐变得如轻纱般透明。
t_en: Lesley's voice is growing weaker, his skin and clothing slowly growing as transparent as light muslin.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的城景若虚若实地映在他的身上——皮肤之下不见骨血，只有灯烛与星光。
t_en: The cityscape of Penacony reflected on his body, half illusion, half true. No blood or bones could be seen under his skin, only candles and starlight.

t_ch: 我感觉身体十分…轻盈，使不上力。意识…好像在变得轻薄，感知有些模糊了。
t_en: My body feels so... light, so powerless. My mind... I feel it growing thin. My senses are becoming dull...

t_ch: 你正在消失…
t_en: You are disappearing...

t_ch: 梦快醒了。
t_en: The dream is over.

t_ch: 但…不知怎么的，我感觉很安心。这种踏实感，我在这梦境中从未有过。
t_en: But... I don't know why, I feel at ease. I've never felt this peace of mind inside the dream world like this.

t_ch: 我好像看见了…看见了…我自己。我身体里的莱斯利•迪恩…他好像在对我微笑，好像在对我说：「做得好」。
t_en: I feel like I can see... I can see... myself. The Lesley Dean inside my body... It's as if he's smiling at me and saying: "Well done."

t_ch: 说来古怪，明明我之前还那么惧怕会失去「自我」——但从您口中得知真相以后，我反而有了放手的勇气。
t_en: It is strange to say it, I was so scared before of losing "myself"... But ever since you told me the truth, I've gained the courage to let go.

t_ch: 和您一起经历了那么多之后…我感到更加坚定了。我知道自己做了正确的事。
t_en: After experiencing all of this with you... I feel even more determined. I know that I have done the right thing.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我能最后再问您一个问题吗？
t_en: {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, may I ask you a final question?

t_ch: 请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 您觉得，在未来…在我消失之后，人们会以何种方式铭记莱斯利•迪恩？
t_en: In the future... After I disappear, how do you think people will remember the name "Lesley Dean"?

t_ch: 他是永不消逝的明星。
t_en: He is the star who will never die.

t_ch: 莱斯利•迪恩——「银河之星」。
t_en: Lesley Dean — "Cosmic Star."

t_ch: 的确，莱斯利•迪恩就是为了银幕而生的。那颗明星曾在夜空中闪耀过，就算转瞬即逝，也总会有人记得它曾经的璀璨。
t_en: Indeed, Lesley Dean was born for the silver screen. That star once shined bright in the sky. Even if it was just a fleeting moment, there are those who would remember his brilliance forever.

t_ch: 他是个多情的人。
t_en: He is a loving man.

t_ch: 莱斯利•迪恩——「宇宙情人」。
t_en: Lesley Dean — "Universal Lover."

t_ch: 在世的短短数十年里，莱斯利•迪恩始终没有学会该如何毫无保留地去爱…即便如此，他仍然被许许多多的人们拥戴着。
t_en: In his brief few decades in this world, Lesley never learned how to love without reservation... Yet, he was supported by so many.

t_ch: 他是个高尚的人。
t_en: He is a noble man.

t_ch: 莱斯利•迪恩——「黄金之心」
t_en: Lesley Dean — "Man with a Heart of Gold."

t_ch: 莱斯利•迪恩也曾身不由己，也曾做出过令他自己后悔的决定…但哪怕被名利场包围，他善良的本心也从未改变。
t_en: Lesley Dean once lived, unable to make his own decisions, and also made many decisions that he regretted... But though he was surrounded by fame and fortune, his kind heart was never changed.

t_ch: 我该感谢那些用梦境构建出「我」的人。他们耗费热情和生命塑造了「我」，我也理应以爱回报他们，帮助他们释怀。
t_en: I should be grateful to those people that used the dreamscape to create "me." They gave their passion and their lives to create "me," so it was my duty to repay them and help them move on.

t_ch: 他们为我创造了一个机会，准许我为莱斯利•迪恩的故事续上一个更好的结尾。
t_en: They created an opportunity for me and allowed me to give Lesley Dean's story a greater ending.

t_ch: 和您经历过这一切以后，我感觉自己真正活过了一回。哪怕只是个错觉，我也会无比珍惜。
t_en: After experiencing all of this with you, I feel like I truly lived. Even if I am wrong, this is something that I will treasure above all.

t_ch: 或许我们以后很难再见了，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}。但——「愿温柔的宇宙祝福你的每一次呼吸，我的朋友。」
t_en: We may never meet again, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. But... "I pray that the gentle universe blesses your every breath, my friend."

t_ch: ——《薄荷色的星河》，我最爱的一部电影。有机会的话，希望您能和所爱之人一同观赏。
t_en: ...My favorite movie is The Mint-Colored Galaxy. If you get an opportunity, I hope you will watch it together with the one you love.

t_ch: 怎么回事？莱斯利先生，上一秒还站在这里…然后「倏」地一下就消失了？
t_en: What's going on? Mr. Lesley was just standing here just a second ago... Then he just suddenly disappeared?

t_ch: 是不是被现实里的人叫醒了？我之前也见过这种情况。
t_en: Was he woken up by someone in reality? That's happened to me before.

t_ch: 啧，梦境就是这点不好！你永远都不知道刚才还在面对面聊天的人是不是下一秒就会被吵醒。
t_en: Tsk, that's the worst thing about the Dreamscape! You never know if the person you're talking to is just going to suddenly be woken up and disappear.

t_ch: 我的签名，只签了一半…唉，等下次吧。不知道莱斯利先生什么时候会再入梦……
t_en: My autograph, it's only half finished... Ah, I'll get it done next time. I wonder when Mr. Lesley will next enter the dream?

t_ch: 是您啊，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}。很高兴能在这座梦想之城与您相遇。
t_en: It's you, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. I'm so happy that I got a chance to meet with you in this City of Dreams.

t_ch: 很抱歉，如您所见，我现在有些…忙碌。您有兴致的话，或许晚些时候我们可以多聊聊。
t_en: Sorry, as you can see, I'm a little... busy. If you're interested, perhaps we could talk properly a little later?

t_ch: 施展钟表把戏
t_en: Here comes the Clockwork.

t_ch: 您就是{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}吧？久仰了，在下是艾弗法先生的秘书，负责本次「晖长石号」的交接事项。
t_en: You must be {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}? I've heard so much about you. I am Mr. Alfalfa's secretary, and I'm responsible for coordinating the handover of the Radiant Feldspar.

t_ch: 你找我有什么事吗？
t_en: How can I help you?

t_ch: 关于「晖长石号」的所有权问题，有些事项还需要与您接洽一二呢。
t_en: Regarding the issue of Radiant Feldspar's ownership, there are some matters that require your attention.

t_ch: …还真是白送我了啊。
t_en: ...Are you guys really giving it to me unconditionally?

t_ch: 是的，根据我方与星穹列车方的协议，「晖长石号」由您来接管。
t_en: Yes. According to the agreement between us and the Astral Express, the Radiant Feldspar will be left in your care.

t_ch: 这明明是大家的共有财产吧…
t_en: But this obviously belongs to everyone...

t_ch: 是的，「晖长石号」的所有权已转移到星穹列车名下，不过…根据最新协商结果——星穹列车方已指定您为此事全权代表。
t_en: Correct. Ownership of the Radiant Feldspar has been transferred to the Astral Express, but... according to the latest agreement's terms... the Astral Express has appointed you as the authoritative representative for this handover.

t_ch: 星穹列车方此前已向我方确认，正式授权您为「晖长石号」的管理者。换句话说，您就是这艘空艇的现任船长——祝贺您！
t_en: The Astral Express has also confirmed with us — they have officially authorized you to be the administrator of the Radiant Feldspar. Which means, you are the present captain of this airship. Congratulations!

t_ch: 接下来，为了确保飞艇的正常运营，您需要聘请一位可靠的大副，由大副来统筹空艇杂务。
t_en: Next, to ensure the airship's proper operation, you need to hire a reliable first mate to organize its daily affairs.

t_ch: 那我该去哪里招人呢？
t_en: Where should I go about hiring such a person?

t_ch: 请您放心，我将全程协助您，招聘事项由我来帮忙筹备，您来决策即可。
t_en: Please do not worry, I will be assisting you the entire way. I'll help organize the recruitment, and you just have to make the final call.

t_ch: 亲力亲为，就别招人了吧…
t_en: Nothing better than doing it myself — I don't think hiring a first mate is necessary...

t_ch: 很抱歉，我知道无名客都身怀绝技，但空艇事务繁杂…还是找位经验丰富的大副对接更稳妥。
t_en: I apologize. I know the Nameless are all masters of their craft, but airship affairs are quite complicated... It's better to look for a first mate with ample experience to handle them.

t_ch: 放心，为了表达我们家系对星穹列车的诚意，我在苜蓿草的人才库中，为您筛选出了几位合适人选——
t_en: Don't worry, as a show of sincerity from The Family to the Astral Express, I have selected a few suitable candidates from the Alfalfa's talent repository...

t_ch: 船长居然这么年轻…
t_en: The captain's so young...

t_ch: 新船长，新气象……
t_en: New captain, new beginnings...

t_ch: 选我选我～
t_en: Pick me, pick me!

t_ch: 我来为您介绍一下——
t_en: Allow me to introduce them...

t_ch: 一号候选人「罗莎」，天环族出身，是业内闻名的「铁娘子」。
t_en: Our number one candidate is Rosa, a Halovian. She is known as the "Iron Lady" in the circle.

t_ch: 二号候选人「冷芯」，优雅的智械，拥有管理学博士学位。
t_en: Number two is Caldwick, an elegant Intellitron, armed with a PhD in Management.

t_ch: 三号候选人「佩德罗」，出身于皮皮西人的航行世家，航行经验丰富。
t_en: The third and final candidate is Pedro, a Pepeshi hailing from a lineage of navigators, and is a veteran spacefarer.

t_ch: 您的选择是？
t_en: Who will you choose?

t_ch: 再多了解一下「罗莎」。
t_en: I'd like to learn more about Rosa.

t_ch: 我对细节的追求无人能及，有机会的话希望能与您共事。
t_en: I am relentless when it comes to the pursuit of detail. I hope to work with you if I get the chance.

t_ch: 罗莎因其精准的细节掌控和雷厉风行的执行力而著称，她总是以雇主的需求优先，曾扬言绝不给雇主增加负担。
t_en: Rosa's meticulousness, vigor, and speed in executing tasks are well known. She always puts her employers first, and once proclaimed that she would never place additional burdens on them.

t_ch: 您确认要雇佣罗莎为您的大副吗？
t_en: Do you wish to recruit Rosa as your first mate?

t_ch: 由于合同条款的限制，<color=#dbc291ff>确定聘用当前大副后，不可更换大副人选。</color>除非大副犯有严重过失，否则随意解雇将面临高额的赔偿费用。这请您注意。
t_en: According to the clause's restrictions imposed by the agreement, <color=#dbc291ff>once you employ the current first mate, you are prohibited from changing your selection.</color> Unless the first mate commits an act of gross misconduct, the unprofessional dismissal of your first mate will incur a massive compensation fee. Please take note of that.

t_ch: 就选你了。
t_en: I'll pick you then.

t_ch: 很好，恭喜您招到心仪的大副！
t_en: Very good. Congrats on hiring your desired first mate!

t_ch: 不，让我再想想
t_en: No, let me think about it.

t_ch: 不急，您可以慢慢考虑。
t_en: There's no hurry. Please take your time.

t_ch: 再多了解一下「冷芯」。
t_en: I'd like to learn more about Caldwick.

t_ch: 在下冷芯，久仰大名，如有荣幸，愿为阁下效劳。
t_en: I am Caldwick, and I have heard about your feats aplenty. If I may be allowed the honor, I would be more than glad to be of service.

t_ch: 冷芯先生，曾连续五年获得航行家协会评选的「最佳上司」称号，证明了他在人才管理领域的卓越成就。
t_en: Mr. Caldwick was awarded the title of "Best Superior" five years in a row by the Navigators Association, proving that he has garnered stellar performances in the domain of talent management.

t_ch: 您确认要雇佣冷芯为您的大副吗？
t_en: Do you wish to recruit Caldwick as your first mate?

t_ch: 由于合同条款的限制，<color=#dbc291ff>确定聘用当前大副后，不可更换大副人选。</color>除非大副犯有严重过失，否则随意解雇将面临高额的赔偿费用。这请您注意。
t_en: According to the clause's restrictions imposed by the agreement, <color=#dbc291ff>once you employ the current first mate, you are prohibited from changing your selection.</color> Unless the first mate commits an act of gross misconduct, the unprofessional dismissal of your first mate will incur a massive compensation fee. Please take note of that.

t_ch: 就选你了。
t_en: I'll pick you then.

t_ch: 很好，恭喜您招到心仪的大副！
t_en: Very good. Congrats on hiring your desired first mate!

t_ch: 不，让我再想想
t_en: No, let me think about it.

t_ch: 不急，您可以慢慢考虑。
t_en: There's no hurry. Please take your time.

t_ch: 再多了解一下「佩德罗」。
t_en: I'd like to learn more about Pedro.

t_ch: 船长大人，不知道我是否有幸在您麾下露几手～
t_en: My captain, I wonder if I'll be lucky enough to flaunt my skills under your command!

t_ch: 佩德罗先生以其坚韧不拔的性格和广泛的人脉闻名，他的能力和影响力在业内也是有目共睹的。
t_en: Mr. Pedro is famed for his tenacious character and extensive network. His capabilities and influence are also outstanding among his peers.

t_ch: 您确认要雇佣佩德罗为您的大副吗？
t_en: Do you wish to recruit Pedro as your first mate?

t_ch: 由于合同条款的限制，<color=#dbc291ff>确定聘用当前大副后，不可更换大副人选。</color>除非大副犯有严重过失，否则随意解雇将面临高额的赔偿费用。这请您注意。
t_en: According to the clause's restrictions imposed by the agreement, <color=#dbc291ff>once you employ the current first mate, you are prohibited from changing your selection.</color> Unless the first mate commits an act of gross misconduct, the unprofessional dismissal of your first mate will incur a massive compensation fee. Please take note of that.

t_ch: 就选你了。
t_en: I'll pick you then.

t_ch: 很好，恭喜您招到心仪的大副！
t_en: Very good. Congrats on hiring your desired first mate!

t_ch: 不，让我再想想
t_en: No, let me think about it.

t_ch: 不急，您可以慢慢考虑。
t_en: There's no hurry. Please take your time.

t_ch: 这位就是您聘用的新任大副，岗前培训也已完成，空艇上还有些交接杂项需要处理，我先行一步，就不打扰二位了。
t_en: Here's your newly appointed first mate. The onboarding training has been completed, but there are still some miscellaneous handover issues that need to be handled on the airship. I'll leave you two to it.

t_ch: 家族秘书向你欠身致意，在征得你同意后施施然离开……
t_en: The Family's secretary bows politely and, upon receiving your approval, steadily departs...

t_ch: 您好，您就是新任船长吧？没想到居然这么年轻…我叫罗莎，很高兴见到您。
t_en: Hello, are you the new captain? I didn't think you'd be this young... I'm Rosa, pleased to meet you.

t_ch: 感谢您对我的信任——我就不浪费您的宝贵时间了，有些工作上的事务要跟您汇报。
t_en: Thank you for your trust in me. I won't waste your precious time. Some official matters require your attention.

t_ch: 首先，我觉得原有的船员素质不错，但还有进步的空间，比如说——他们的制服与工作分区并不统一，作为只招待名流贵宾的空艇，这实在是有些失礼。
t_en: Firstly, I think the existing crew's sense of professionalism is decent, but there is room for improvement. For example, their uniforms do not match their job's roles and responsibilities. As an airship that services distinguished guests, this is a slight of manners.

t_ch: 所以您我希望您能同意，由我来管理船员，制定行为准则……
t_en: I hope you will agree to my request to manage the crewmates and draw up a code of conduct...

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 我一定不会让您失望的，放心吧。
t_en: I won't disappoint you. Don't worry.

t_ch: 原则上可以，别太苛刻就是了。
t_en: I'll acquiesce on principle. Just go easy on them.

t_ch: 放心，我不是那种人。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm not that kind of person.

t_ch: 细节决定成败，请船长放心，我早就做好预案了，我保证这会让空艇的服务质量再上一层！
t_en: Details decide success or failure. Don't worry, captain, I've already prepared a draft proposal. I guarantee this will elevate the airship's service quality to new heights!

t_ch: 你见识到了罗莎的雷厉风行——
t_en: You have certainly witnessed Rosa's whirlwind-like vigor...

t_ch: 一段时间过后，她带着新招的船员，过来向你汇报她的工作结果。
t_en: After a while, with the newly hired crew members behind her, she comes before you to report the state of her work.

t_ch: 船长好！
t_en: Hi, captain!

t_ch: 船长好！
t_en: Hi, captain!

t_ch: 船长好！
t_en: Hi, captain!

t_ch: 船长好，很荣幸能向您汇报现阶段的初步成果——这些新来的年轻人还不错，已经能适应空艇的工作节奏了。
t_en: Hello captain, I'm honored to report the current phase's initial results to you — these young newcomers are coming along fine, and are getting used to the airship's current work pace.

t_ch: 这排场看着还不错。
t_en: This lineup looks pretty good.

t_ch: 专业的团队才会给我们的贵宾留下深刻的印象。
t_en: Only the most professional of teams will leave a lasting impression on our VIP guests.

t_ch: 把我喊来就为了这点事啊…
t_en: Did you call for me for such a trivial matter...?

t_ch: 抱歉，稍后我会向您汇报其他事项。
t_en: Apologies, I shall inform you on other matters in a while.

t_ch: 这次来，其实还有件事要向您汇报，还请您跟我实地看看比较好——
t_en: I just have something else to report to you at present. It's better if I showed you in person...

t_ch: 罗莎带着你来到了甲板上。
t_en: Rosa takes you to the deck.

t_ch: 不知道您注意到没有——空艇换了主人，却还在用「晖长石号」的旧称，空艇尾翼也没有涂装……
t_en: I don't know if you've noticed — the airship has a new owner now, but it's still called the Radiant Feldspar. And the airship's empennage isn't spray-painted...

t_ch: 这在我看来是完全不可接受，您初来乍到，就该打响自己的旗号才是。
t_en: This is, to me, simply unacceptable. You should be flying your own flag that heralds your glorious appointment.

t_ch: 我建议您为未来的经营方向定个调，把飞艇的涂装定下来，不知您意下如何？
t_en: I suggest that you set the tone for future endeavors, and decide on a design for the airship. What do you think?

t_ch: 把我的模样涂上去吧！
t_en: Spray-paint my face on it!

t_ch: 我得承认，您确实相貌不凡——但按照业内旧例，涂在尾翼的一般都是吉祥物…这未免有失您的身份。
t_en: I admit that your visage is extraordinary, but according to tradition, it's usually a mascot that's painted on the tail... Your idea might perhaps be beneath your station.

t_ch: 用我的名字命名吧！
t_en: I'll name it after myself then!

t_ch: 唔…「晖长石号」换成「{NICKNAME}号」…以人化物，恐怕会招来非议。
t_en: Mmm... The "Radiant Feldspar" renamed to the "{NICKNAME}"... that might invite criticism, presenting you as an object.

t_ch: 你有什么好的建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any good suggestions?

t_ch: 容我想想……
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 您有什么喜欢的宠物或者吉祥物吗？
t_en: Do you have any pets or mascots you like?

t_ch: …列车长帕姆。
t_en: ...Pom-Pom, Conductor of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 唔，听上去是个不错的选择，能凸显星穹列车对空艇的所有权。
t_en: Mm, that doesn't sound like a bad idea at all. It represents the authority of the Astral Express holds over the airship.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「开拓之尾号」</color>，在尾翼上涂上帕姆的模样，跟随着列车长的尾巴，就永远不会迷航。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Trailblaze's Stern</color>. Pom-Pom's image will be painted on the empennage — we'll never get lost in the stars if we follow the conductor's trail.

t_ch: …我喜欢垃圾桶。
t_en: ...I like trashcans.

t_ch: 虽然是垃圾桶，但这个形象莫名地给我一种英勇无畏的感觉，在梦里反倒有一种强烈的个人风格，这很难得。
t_en: A trashcan might be unorthodox, but that visual imbues an inexplicable sense of heroic staunchness in me — an intense personal style that stands out in a dream. This is a rare feeling.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「塔塔洛夫号」</color>——传说中伟大的垃圾之王，它将指引我们走向征服和伟业！用涂装来彰显您的硬实力和威慑力。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Tatalov</color> — the grand Garbage King of legend. It will guide us toward conquest and majestic grandeur! Give it a coat of paint to demonstrate your show of force and intimidation.

t_ch: …果然还是钟表小子。
t_en: ...I guess it still has to be Clockie.

t_ch: 不错，匹诺康尼特色，而且…也不用担心有版权问题。
t_en: Not bad, a Penaconian hallmark, and... no worries about copyright infringement.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「飞翔时针号」</color>——继承了钟表小子的意志，听上去就是个了不得的正义组织。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Soaring Clock Hand</color> — an inheritance of Clockie's will. It sounds like a high-flying organization of justice.

t_ch: 看来您已经做出明智的选择，接下来的事情就交给我来处理吧。
t_en: Looks like you've already made the most astute choice. Leave the rest of the matter to me.

t_ch: 罗莎吩咐船员们对空艇尾翼进行了涂装。
t_en: Rosa instructs the crew members to paint the airship's empennage.

t_ch: 很有视觉冲击力的涂装，不愧是您！如果您之后对空艇的名称与涂装不满意，可以跟我说，我会帮您进行更换的。
t_en: A visually striking coat of paint. It's got your style written all over it! If you're dissatisfied with the airship's name and paint in the future, you can tell me, and I'll help you change them.

t_ch: 不过，由于预算有限，目前只按照您的喜好，做了三种主题的设计，请您见谅。
t_en: But we have a limited budget, so we could only produce three design themes according to your preferences. I hope you won't mind.

t_ch: 而且…光有这些还是不够的，您还是太低调啦。身为新任船长，有些地方的必须亮出您的招牌才行呢。
t_en: And... these meager changes aren't enough. You're still not broadcasting yourself well. As the new captain, there are some areas that require flying your banner, so to speak.

t_ch: 比如说…这里……
t_en: Like... here...

t_ch: 罗莎将你带至空艇游人必经的长廊里。
t_en: Rosa leads you to the long gallery on the airship where visitors must pass by.

t_ch: 这个地方…依我看，应该摆上您的雕像才好，好叫来往客人和船员们，都能一目了然：谁才是这艘空艇的主人。
t_en: This place... I'd add your statue to it if it were up to me. Passing visitors and crew members will instantly understand at a glance: This is the master of this airship.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的折纸打印技术相当成熟，您需要现打一座半身像摆上去吗？
t_en: Penacony's origami printing techniques are quite advanced. Do you want to print a bust tailored to your visage and place it here?

t_ch: 开摆开摆！
t_en: Do it!

t_ch: 遵命！
t_en: Aye-aye, Cap'n!

t_ch: 帮我弄好看点。
t_en: Make me look nicer, please.

t_ch: 放心，我们天环族的审美绝不会令您失望。
t_en: Don't worry, you'll never go wrong with a Halovian's aesthetics.

t_ch: 嗯哼，太合适了，完美契合。
t_en: Mm-hmm, excellent, a perfect fit.

t_ch: 最后还有一件事，也请您跟我稍挪步，以便视察——
t_en: There's one last thing. If you follow me, you can see for yourself...

t_ch: 罗莎将你带至船长室，她示意你看向空白的墙壁。
t_en: Rosa takes you to the captain's cabin, and gestures for you to look at the blank wall.

t_ch: 请您注意看，这里——
t_en: Please, look over here...

t_ch: 墙上空空荡荡的，这怎么行…既然是船长室，就该按照传统，把您的画像挂上去。这样才能体现您的身份呐。
t_en: The wall is starkly empty. This won't do... A captain's cabin should honor tradition by having your portrait hung high. It's the only way to declare your status.

t_ch: 画像我已经替您准备好了…不过…具体要不要挂，还是要听从您的指示。
t_en: I've already prepared your portrait for you... but... whether it should be hung up is your decision.

t_ch: 火速把我挂上去吧！
t_en: Chop-chop, then!

t_ch: 遵命！
t_en: Aye-aye, Cap'n!

t_ch: …我也没有别的选项啊。
t_en: ...You make it sound like I had a choice to begin with.

t_ch: 嗯？「选项」？噢，您是说「选择」啊。不不不，您执意不挂的话也不是不行，只不过我感觉这样就太低调啦，做人还是不能太内秀啊。
t_en: Hmm? Voice? Oh, you meant choice. No, no, if you're insistent on leaving it empty, that's fine too. I just think it's unbecoming for someone of your station. It never hurts to flaunt a little.

t_ch: 罗莎捧着你的画像，将它稳稳当当地挂在墙壁上。
t_en: Rosa steadily hangs your portrait on the wall.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，角度没有偏差，画像也很清晰，就该这样才是。
t_en: Mm-mm, the angle's all right, and the portrait looks sharp too. This is how things should be.

t_ch: 船长，空艇的启动事项业已准备完毕，现在我得回到我的工作岗位准备航行事项了，我就在门外随时待命，如有需要，请随意差遣。
t_en: Captain, preparation procedures for the airship's launch are complete. I have to return to my station to get ready for the upcoming journey. I'll be awaiting orders outside the door, so don't hesitate to dispatch me if you have any needs.

t_ch: 尊敬的船长，我叫冷芯，很荣幸能成为您的大副。
t_en: My esteemed captain, I am Caldwick — it is an honor to be your first mate.

t_ch: 有几件船务事项我必须向您汇报，这是在来空艇之处我注意到的问题——
t_en: There are certain ship-related matters I must report to you — these are problems I noticed when I set foot on the airship...

t_ch: 第一件事是，我准备了一套管理准则，我希望能在空艇上一一践行，希望能允许我这么做。
t_en: The first being, I have prepared a set of management guidelines which I hope will be adhered to while aboard the airship. Please grant my request.

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 感谢您的信任。
t_en: Thank you for your trust.

t_ch: 你的准则是？
t_en: What are these guidelines about?

t_ch: 我的准则基于信任与对人性的洞察，我希望能给予船员们足够的职权自由。
t_en: They are based on trust, as well as my insights on humanity. I hope they can provide sufficient professional freedom for the crew members.

t_ch: 我相信给予船员自主的空间能激发他们的潜力和创造力。只要能完成任务，他们可以有更多的自由来安排自己的工作方式。
t_en: I believe in giving the crew members their space with hopes of inspiring them to realize their own potential and creativity. As long as they are able to accomplish their task, they can have more freedom to organize their personal working style.

t_ch: 基于我的过往经验，这会大大提升了大家的工作效率，还能增强团队的凝聚力——船员们因为感受到被信任，通常会展现出更高的责任感和工作热情。
t_en: From my own experiences, this will immensely increase productivity for the crew as well as team bonding — from the trust given to them, they will often perform with elevated enthusiasm and show a greater sense of responsibility.

t_ch: 所以我恳请您能授权予我，让我按照这项管理准则试运行一段时间……
t_en: So, I implore you to allow me to follow this management style for a trial period...

t_ch: 放手去做吧，我相信你能行。
t_en: Go ahead. I believe in you.

t_ch: …那就试试吧。
t_en: ...Let's try it.

t_ch: 船长，冷芯感谢您对我的信任与支持。我将按照这套准则运行一段时间，然后根据实际效果进行评估和调整。我相信，您很快就能看到积极的结果。
t_en: Captain, thank you sincerely for your trust in me. I'll follow this guideline for a while, then evaluate and adjust the plan based off the outcome. I'm confident that you'll see satisfactory results soon.

t_ch: 一段时间后，冷芯要求向你汇报工作结果。
t_en: After some time, Caldwick requests to report in with his results.

t_ch: 大副人还怪好的。
t_en: The first mate is a pretty nice guy.

t_ch: 船长这模样真俊。
t_en: The captain is so cool.

t_ch: 这就是传说中的船长吗？
t_en: Is this the legendary captain?

t_ch: 船长，很高兴见到您。我根据自己的管理准则招聘了新的船员。
t_en: Captain, it's an honor to see you again. I've recruited new crew members based on my own management guidelines.

t_ch: 结果怎么样？
t_en: How are the results?

t_ch: 以住客的反馈为基准，可以说是好评如潮。
t_en: The feedback from our guests is overflowing with praise!

t_ch: 看起来什么人都有…
t_en: Looks like there are all sorts of people here...

t_ch: 我更重视他们的主观能动性，而不是他们的外表或制服。
t_en: I value their initiative more than their appearance or uniform.

t_ch: 船员们因为获得了更多的自由和信任，工作表现出色，空艇上的客人们都给予了好评。
t_en: With the freedom and trust given to the crew, their performance level has increased, and the guests on the airship have been showering us with compliments.

t_ch: 除此以外，还意外拿到了一位知名旅游博主的大力推荐。种种事项，皆已证明了我的管理准则有效性。
t_en: Additionally, we've also gotten a glowing recommendation from a famous travel influencer! All these events prove the effectiveness of my management guidelines.

t_ch: 基于我们的成功，我建议我们对空艇进一步改变——有几位船员主动向我反馈了一件事，他们有一处地方有些问题，恐怕需要您和我实地走访一下。
t_en: With this successful endeavor in hand, I'd like to suggest a few improvements to be made to the airship — a couple of crew members have come forth with feedback on an issue they're facing in a certain area. I'll need your help to conduct a site visit with me.

t_ch: 冷芯带着你来到了甲板上。
t_en: Caldwick takes you to the deck.

t_ch: 船员们向我提出建议：应当更换空艇尾翼的涂装，更换空艇的旧称，以便凸显空艇易主后的变化。
t_en: This is the suggestion given by the crew: It's time to change the empennage and the name of the airship to denote the changes that have taken place since the airship's change of hands.

t_ch: 我相信这将进一步提升我们的品牌形象，不知您是如何看待此事的？
t_en: I believe this change will be able to elevate our brand image. Can I get your opinion on this?

t_ch: 直接用我的名字我的脸。
t_en: Just use my name and face.

t_ch: 很抱歉，空艇的名字要被用作商业宣传，直接采用您的形象和姓名恐怕会有不小的风险。此事请恕我反对。
t_en: I'm sorry. The airship's name will be used for commercial purposes. If we use your image and name directly, it poses too much of a risk. I'll have to reject that proposal, and beg your understanding.

t_ch: 你有什么好的建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any good suggestions?

t_ch: 能吸引客人眼球，又能凸显您风格的，便是最好的设计。
t_en: The best design must be attractive to guests and emphasize your style.

t_ch: 您是船长，当以您的审美喜好为重，不知您是否有什么喜欢的人或事，可以用作空艇的吉祥物形象？
t_en: You're the captain. It's of utmost importance to consider your aesthetic preferences. Do you have a favorite person or thing to use as the mascot of the airship?

t_ch: …列车长帕姆。
t_en: ...Pom-Pom, Conductor of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 星穹列车的列车长吗？在尾翼上涂上帕姆的模样，是个不错的选择。
t_en: The Astral Express' conductor? It'll be a nice touch to paint a Pom-Pom lookalike on the airship's empennage.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「开拓之尾号」</color>，强调列车长帕姆的地位，凸出您无名客的身份。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Trailblaze's Stern</color>, emphasizing the importance of conductor Pom-Pom and highlighting your status as a Nameless.

t_ch: …垃圾桶。
t_en: ...Trashcan.

t_ch: 虽然是垃圾桶，但是在梦里，越是反常，反而越能抓人眼球。
t_en: Even though it's a trashcan, the more unconventional something appears in dreams, the more captivating it becomes.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「塔塔洛夫号」</color>，它是传说中伟大的垃圾之王，它的模样极具前瞻性，兼有后现代的艺术感。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Tatalov</color>. It is the legendary Garbage King, and it has a forward-looking feel with a post-modern aesthetic.

t_ch: …钟表小子。
t_en: ...Clockie.

t_ch: 噢，匹诺康尼人尽皆知的形象，这可以节省一笔宣传费用。
t_en: Oh, by using a well-known image in Penacony. This will save a fortune in promotional costs.

t_ch: 那<color=#dbc291ff>空艇就可以命名为：「飞翔时针号」</color>，由钟表小子领航的空艇，足以配上空艇住客的身份。
t_en: Then <color=#dbc291ff>the airship's new name shall henceforth be the Soaring Clock Hand</color> — only with an airship helmed by Clockie is worthy of its guests.

t_ch: 非常感谢您的支持，船长。请稍等——
t_en: Thank you for your support, captain. Please hold on for a moment...

t_ch: 冷芯吩咐船员们对空艇尾翼进行了涂装。
t_en: Caldwick instructs the crew to repaint the empennage of the airship.

t_ch: 我已用梦泡将您内心的提案记录起来——也包含备选方案，以便未来更换涂装之需。
t_en: I've used a dream bubble to record your preferred proposal — it also includes different options to prepare for the possibility of future repaints.

t_ch: 尾翼的涂装工作已完成，更换的船名也跟船员们同步出去了，大家对此都很满意。
t_en: The paint job for the empennage is complete. The crew has been informed of the name change and everyone is happy with it.

t_ch: 此外，我还收到了船员们的一个反馈——他们想在长廊摆放您的雕像，以示敬意。
t_en: Also, I've received feedback that the crew members wish to honor you by placing your statue in the promenade gallery.

t_ch: 船长，请随我来。
t_en: Captain, please follow me.

t_ch: 冷芯将你带至空艇游人必经的长廊里。
t_en: Caldwick leads you to the long gallery where visitors must pass by.

t_ch: 此处平时人来人往，但凡登上空艇，无论去哪儿，这里都是必经之路。
t_en: It's usually bustling here. Once you board the airship, you'll have to pass by here no matter where you're going.

t_ch: 船员们提议，就在此处摆上您的半身像，我认为这是一个展示我们团队对您领导的尊敬和信任的好方式。同时，这也能加强团队的凝聚力。
t_en: The crew suggests that we place a statue of your bust here. I think it's a great way for our team to show our respect and trust in your leadership. Additionally, it will strengthen our team's unity.

t_ch: 先前新招了不少人，其中有位折纸大学出身的船员，他已经为您试着打印了雕像。不知道您是否同意将它摆在这里？
t_en: We've had many new recruits, one among them having graduated from the Paperfold University. He has already tried printing your statue. I wonder if you're agreeable to having it be placed here?

t_ch: 快端上来吧！
t_en: Bring it up!

t_ch: 请您稍等。
t_en: Just a moment.

t_ch: 不好看的话就别摆了。
t_en: Don't put it up if it's not nice.

t_ch: 请您放心，我已先行检阅过，他的审美相当不错，足以将您的容貌还原出六七成。
t_en: Please be rest assured. I've checked it. His aesthetics are good. He's able to replicate at least sixty percent of your appearance.

t_ch: 无论看过多少次，当再次看到的时候还是觉得很震撼，雕像的清新感，是业内所有船长都无法媲美的……
t_en: No matter how many times I've seen it, I'm still taken aback. The statue looks refreshing, unrivaled by any captain in the industry...

t_ch: 抱歉，方才太过专注于欣赏雕像成品，有些失态了。
t_en: Sorry, I was mesmerized by the statue. That was inappropriate.

t_ch: 我还有最后一件事想向您汇报，有位船员在对船长室进行日常清洁时，发现了一个问题——请您跟我到现场看看吧。
t_en: There's just one last thing to report: A crew member discovered an issue during routine cleaning of the captain's cabin. Please follow me to take a look.

t_ch: 冷芯将你带至船长室，示意你看向空白的墙壁。
t_en: Caldwick takes you to the captain's cabin, signaling you to look at the blank wall.

t_ch: 船员的建议在这里挂上一幅您的画像，在您离航时，也能被所有船员看到。我们是一个团队，大家都想见到您——无论以什么样的方式。
t_en: The crew member suggests hanging a painting of you here. When you leave the airship, your presence will still be felt here. We are a team. Everyone wishes to see you, no matter the form.

t_ch: 提出这个建议的船员之前曾师从某位油画大师，对无名客的传奇倾慕已久，因而早早画下了您的画像。是否要挂上去，请您自由决断。
t_en: The crew member who suggested this was an apprentice of a master painter who also long-admired the legends of the Nameless, and had your portrait painted long ago. Please feel free to decide whether you want to hang up the painting.

t_ch: 快挂上去让我看看！
t_en: Quickly, hang it up for me to see!

t_ch: 请您稍等。
t_en: Just a moment.

t_ch: …船员们怎么什么都会。
t_en: ...Wow, our crew can do anything.

t_ch: 您说笑了，基于管理准则，我允许大家展现自己的特长，只是恰好遇到会画的人罢了。
t_en: You jest. According to management guidelines, I allow people to showcase their specialties. It was pure coincidence that we met someone who could draw.

t_ch: 冷芯捧着你的画像，将它稳稳当当地挂在墙壁上。
t_en: Caldwick holds your portrait and hangs it on the wall securely.

t_ch: 希望您会喜欢这幅画像。
t_en: I hope you'll enjoy this painting.

t_ch: 船长，目前大家的反馈与空艇杂务我已汇报完毕，我平时就在舵轮附近待命，有事的话我会再跟您汇报的。回见。
t_en: I've completed my report on all the feedback from the crew and the administrative staff of the airship. Usually, I'll be standing by the wheel of the airship. I'll report back if anything comes up. Until we meet again.

t_ch: 您就是船长？我叫佩德罗，是您新招的大副，无名客的事迹我听说了，我最佩服您这种了不起的大人物！
t_en: You're the captain? Call me Pedro, I'm your new first mate. I've heard of the Nameless. I'm in awe of you!

t_ch: 我家里世代航行，算是积累了些家传经验。所以我来这空艇上走了一遭，我就感觉到有些地方可以改进。
t_en: I come from a family that has been flying for generations, and I've inherited a bunch of experience from my folks. I've made my rounds on the airship, and I feel that there is some room for improvement.

t_ch: 第一个需要改进的地方，是船员本身的培训。当然，这是上一任船长的遗留问题了。所以我想向您讨个方便，让我来负责招募与管理船员！
t_en: The first area for improvement: Crew member training. Of course, this is a legacy issue left behind by the previous captain. I'd like to ask for a favor of letting me be in charge of recruiting and managing the crew!

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 太感谢了！
t_en: Thank you so much!

t_ch: 我也没有反对的选项啊。
t_en: It doesn't look like I have the option to refuse...

t_ch: 什么项？这是什么时髦的新词吗？抱歉我没听懂，但您的意思应该是同意吧？
t_en: Sorry, my ears must be failing me. You agree to my request, then?

t_ch: 谢谢您的授权，我这就去办！
t_en: Thank you for the approval. I'll get on it!

t_ch: 过了一段时间，忙完招聘事项的佩德罗主动求见。
t_en: Some time later, Pedro, finished with recruitment, asks to meet with you.

t_ch: 老叔说的船长来了…
t_en: It's the captain that uncle mentioned...

t_ch: 别给佩叔丢脸！
t_en: Don't embarrass Uncle Pedro!

t_ch: 船长大人，又见面了。我这次回家招了些人过来，请您检阅！
t_en: Captain, we meet again. I've recruited some folks from my hometown. Please take a look!

t_ch: 不是我吹嘘哈，您可以去问问咱们同行——虽说他们跟我都是同一个家族的旁支出身，但我保证他们都是老手，经验和品行都过硬！
t_en: I'm not tooting my own horn. You can ask around in the industry. Although they are from an offshoot of my family, I can guarantee that they are all veterans, packed with experience and integrity.

t_ch: 我航行少，你不要骗我。
t_en: I don't have much experience in flying, so don't trick me.

t_ch: 果真吗？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 那当然！别的不说，咱们皮皮西人是天生的维修好手。我带的这几个家伙，各个都有家族认证的专业技能证书，也是被苜蓿草家系核验过的。
t_en: Of course! Not to mention, we Pepeshi are natural repairmen. These folks that I'm bringing on board all hold official certificates to verify their abilities, issued by The Family themselves! They were verified by the Alfalfa Family as well.

t_ch: 我们的团结与过硬的专业技能，是保证航行成功的关键。能为您效力，我们深感荣幸。船长，我们将竭尽所能为您服务！
t_en: Our unity and world-class professional skills are the key to guaranteeing a successful voyage. We are deeply honored to be under your employment. Captain, please allow us to serve you with everything we've got!

t_ch: 口说无凭，就让我带您去个地方，您就知道了——这还是我大侄子跟我反馈的，我想着正好跟您汇报一下。
t_en: Instead of describing it, let me bring you to a place, and you'll know — my nephew told me about it, and I thought it'd be good to report it to you.

t_ch: 佩德罗带着你来到了甲板上。
t_en: Pedro takes you to the deck.

t_ch: 我的子侄发现尾翼的涂装还没更换，就连「晖长石号」的旧称也没改，这都易主了，是不是要改一下？
t_en: My nephew realized that the paint job on the empennage hasn't been changed, nor has the old name, Radiant Feldspar. Since you're its new master, isn't it time to change it?

t_ch: 您觉得呢？
t_en: What do you think?

t_ch: 大大方方地用我的形象吧。
t_en: Proudly use my image!

t_ch: 哎，这可不行，尾翼多脏啊，航行时万一遇到点事儿，还会破损开裂，涂装用您的模样，不吉利啊。
t_en: Ah, that's a big no-no. The empennage is filthy, and if an accident happens during flying, it'll be split open and ruin your image. That's bad luck!

t_ch: 你有什么好的建议吗？
t_en: Do you have any good suggestions?

t_ch: 容我想想。
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 您是船长，以您的喜好为重，您可有喜欢的人或事？最好是什么萌宠、吉祥物之类，男女老少都会喜欢的形象？
t_en: You're the captain, so it's all about what you like! Do you have a person or thing that you like? Best if it's a cute pet, or a mascot, something everyone will like.

t_ch: …列车长帕姆。
t_en: ...Pom-Pom, Conductor of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 星穹列车的列车长吗？可以可以，看到它的脸，大家就会知道这是无名客的地盘！
t_en: The Conductor of the Astral Express? Not a problem. Looking at this face, everyone will know that this is the turf of the Nameless!

t_ch: 那么，就把这艘空艇重新起名为<color=#dbc291ff>「开拓之尾号」吧</color>——船如其名，只要一直盯着列车长的尾巴，我们就永远不会迷航！
t_en: Then, we'll rename the airship to <color=#dbc291ff>the Trailblaze's Stern</color>! Just like its namesake, as long as we keep our eyes on the conductor's tail, we'll never get lost!

t_ch: …垃圾桶。
t_en: ...Trashcan.

t_ch: …我直说吧，有点奇葩，但我很喜欢，平凡的东西配不上船长您。
t_en: ...To be honest, it's a little weird, but I love it. After all, you're above ordinary things, captain.

t_ch: 那么，就把这艘空艇重新起名为<color=#dbc291ff>「塔塔洛夫号」</color>吧——传说中伟大的垃圾之王，它将指引我们走向征服和伟业！
t_en: Then, we'll rename the airship to <color=#dbc291ff>the Tatalov</color>! The legendary Garbage King will lead us on a path of conquest and greatness!

t_ch: …钟表小子。
t_en: ...Clockie.

t_ch: 唔，的确是男女老少合家欢的首选。要我说，没毛病！
t_en: Well, it's certainly a family favorite for all ages. I'd say, no issue!

t_ch: 那么，就把这艘空艇重新起名为<color=#dbc291ff>「飞翔时针号」</color>吧——继承钟表小子的意志，我们将从上空捍卫美梦小镇的和平！
t_en: Then, we'll rename the airship to the <color=#dbc291ff>Soaring Clock Hand</color>! Inheriting the spirit of Clockie, we will defend the peace of Dreamville from the skies!

t_ch: 我有个研究梦泡的堂兄，给了我一个装置，能记录人脑海中的细化出来的一些意象，拿来做涂装倒是方便。
t_en: I have a cousin who studies dream bubbles. He gave me a device that can record detailed imagery from human minds. Handy when needed for painting.

t_ch: 稍等，我捣鼓下这玩意……
t_en: Hang on, let me figure out this thing...

t_ch: 佩德罗吩咐船员们对空艇尾翼进行了涂装。
t_en: Pedro instructs the crew to paint the airship's empennage.

t_ch: 搞定，不过…这梦泡装置空间有限，只记录了您方才脑海中所构思的三种预案。
t_en: Done, but... This dream bubble has limited space. It's only able to record three ideas you have in your mind.

t_ch: 您以后要是想换涂装，随时跟我说就好。
t_en: If you want to change the design in the future, just let me know.

t_ch: 涂装和更换船名的事项我跟大家说了，受到大家的一致好评。
t_en: I've informed everyone about the change of the paint and the airship's name. They are all singing praises.

t_ch: 除去了一桩未了事，还有一桩——船长，根据我的航行经验，我发现船上有个地方有点问题，您最好来亲自看一下。
t_en: Now that this is done and dusted, there's another issue. Captain, according to my navigational experience, I've realized another problem with the ship. I think it's better if you see it for yourself.

t_ch: 请随我来——
t_en: Please follow me...

t_ch: 佩德罗将你带至空艇游人必经的长廊里。
t_en: Pedro leads you to the long gallery that every visitor must pass by.

t_ch: 船长，这个长廊位于船上的中心位置，每个经过的人都会注意到它。但这里空空荡荡的，如果只摆些工艺品，我感觉这太庸俗了。
t_en: Captain, this long gallery is situated at the heart of the ship. Every visitor that passes by will notice it, but it's currently empty — and it'd look quite boorish if we only decorate it with art pieces.

t_ch: 我想来想去，感觉在这里放置您的半身像最合适。
t_en: I gave it some thought, and I feel that it's most suitable to place a bust of you here.

t_ch: 我有个兄弟，以前在折纸大学念过书，折纸技术一流，他试着为您打印了一座雕像。您看看，要不要摆在这儿试试？
t_en: I have a friend who is a graduate of the Paperfold University, and his origami skills are top-notch. He has tried to print a statue for you. Here, take a look. Why don't we try to place it here?

t_ch: 我等不及要看了！
t_en: I can't wait to see it!

t_ch: 摆出来试试呗。
t_en: Put it here and we'll see.

t_ch: 稍等，等我摆一下……
t_en: Hang on, let me display it...

t_ch: 哦对了，差点忘了最后还有件事得跟你汇报。
t_en: Oh right, almost forgot the last thing that I have to report.

t_ch: 先前在船长室门口跟你汇报招聘事项的时候，我注意到一个问题——请您跟我到现场看看吧。
t_en: Previously, when I reported in about recruitment, I noticed an issue with your room — please follow me to take a look.

t_ch: 佩德罗将你带至船长室，示意你看向空白的墙壁。
t_en: Pedro brings you to the captain's cabin, signaling you to look at the blank wall.

t_ch: 这里不是船长室嘛，之前我来替您准备交接事项时，发现这里空了一块。
t_en: This is the captain's cabin, no? When I came to prepare for your handover, I realized that it has a blank area.

t_ch: 既然是船长室，就该放您的画像，这样一来，别人来跟您谈事，你就往沙发上一坐，背后是您的画像，这样才够威严。
t_en: Since it's the captain's cabin, your portrait should be hanging there. That way, when people come in for discussions, you'll be sitting on the sofa, with your painting behind you, that'll show em' who's boss!

t_ch: 托您的福，苜蓿草家系的那位秘书为您准备了一幅画像，您看要不要摆上去？
t_en: The secretary from the Alfalfa Family has prepared your portrait as a sign of graciousness. Do you want to hang it up?

t_ch: 摆啊，为什么不摆？
t_en: Of course we are hanging it up, pronto!

t_ch: 别急，这就开摆。
t_en: No rush. We'll do it immediately.

t_ch: 他们还挺会做人的。
t_en: How very gracious of them.

t_ch: 举手之劳嘛，这种小忙是我我也愿意帮。
t_en: It was a piece of cake. I'd be willing to help out too for something like this.

t_ch: 佩德罗捧着你的画像，将它稳稳当当地挂在墙壁上。
t_en: Pedro holds your painting and steadily hangs it on the wall.

t_ch: 太好了，我感觉这是房间里的一大亮点。听我老佩德罗的准没错！
t_en: Awesome. I think this is the cherry on top for the room! Listen to Old Pedro and nothing will go wrong!

t_ch: 船长，我已经向您汇报了所有船务事项，我现在回去继续工作了，您要是有事，随时喊我，我就在外面值班。再见！
t_en: Captain, I'm done reporting about all the airship's tasks. I'm gonna head back to work. If something comes up, just gimme a holler! I'll be working outside. See ya!

t_ch: 你细致地审视这幅半身像，发现雕塑家精心捕捉了你的每一个细节，就连头发丝都透露出领导者的睿智。
t_en: You scrutinize the bust and realize that the sculptor has meticulously captured every detail of you — even the strands of hair reveal the wisdom of a leader.

t_ch: 你的雕像被放置在长廊的中心位置，仿佛是船上的守护神，默默注视着过往的船员和客人，传递着一种无形的力量。
t_en: Your statue is placed at the central location of the long gallery, like a guardian of the ship, silently watching over the passing crew and guests, radiating an unseen strength.

t_ch: 或许随着时间的流逝，这尊雕像会成船上的一个话题，进而演化成乃至一个传说。你不敢想象你在传说中会有怎样荒腔走板的事迹。
t_en: Perhaps, as time passes, this statue will become a topic of banter on the ship, eventually evolving into a fable. You dare not even imagine how absurd your story will end up in these tales.

t_ch: 船长，您站着别动，让我瞧瞧……
t_en: Captain, don't move, let me see...

t_ch: 啊…您这模样怎么长的？{F#好美}{M#好帅}的一张脸。这半身像做得真不错，还原了您起码七成的美貌，看着还挺威严！
t_en: Ah... How did you even get this face? It's so {F#beautiful}{M#handsome}. This bust's quality is outstanding — it's managed to capture at least <unbreak>70%</unbreak> of your visage. So stately!

t_ch: 船长…你抱着双臂试试…哈，果然和画像一样。画像里的您文静些，您本人看起来有血有肉的更有活力。
t_en: Captain... try crossing your arms... Ha, looks just like the portrait. You look more demure in it, but you are much more spirited in person.

t_ch: 船长，请您站在这里，让我比对着雕像看看，是否有什么细节问题……
t_en: Captain, please stand here beside the statue so I can see if there are any issues with the details...

t_ch: 唔，五官一致，和您本人一样{F#美丽}{M#帅气}…您看着真年轻，这雕像减少了部分细节，反倒显得气势十足！
t_en: Mm, the facial features are identical, just as {F#beautiful}{M#handsome} as the real thing... You look so young. This statue lacks some details, but it makes you look more imposing in contrast!

t_ch: 您能模仿画像的姿势试试吗？我想检查下画像的细节是否有误。
t_en: Can you imitate the pose in the portrait? I would like to see if there are any mistakes with the details.

t_ch: 可惜纸面上的东西未能百分百还原出您的气势，不过这样也不错……
t_en: A pity that your aura can't be duplicated wholly on paper, but this is fine too...

t_ch: 哎哟，这模样可真俊啊，都赶得上真人了。
t_en: Oh-ho, this look is outstanding. Almost like the real deal.

t_ch: 船长，您转个身我看看…哇哦……
t_en: Captain, please turn around for me... Wow...

t_ch: 您真高…呃呃，不对，您真好看…这雕像也就只有您的六成美貌吧。不过摆着倒是挺霸气的，不愧是船长大人。
t_en: You're so tall... Oh, that's not right, you're so good-looking... This statue only captures about <unbreak>60%</unbreak> of your beauty, but it still looks rather commanding anyway. That's my captain.

t_ch: 船长，你把胳膊抱起来试试——呃，和画像的动作一样…对，就是这样！
t_en: Captain, could you try posing with your arms crossed... Yes, exactly like the painting... That's it, just like that!

t_ch: 哈哈，老佩德罗觉得挺好的，这未免也太{F#好看}{M#帅}了…我都想搞一副油画挂家里试试……
t_en: Haha, I think it's pretty good. This is just too {F#good-looking}{M#handsome}... Even I want a portrait to hang up at home...

t_ch: （从今天起，本{F#小姐}{M#少爷}就算是这座酒店的主人了！）
t_en: (From today onwards, I will be the {F#mistress}{M#master} of this hotel!)

t_ch: （是时候开始整顿匹诺康尼的职场环境了…酒店大堂就是我的第一站。）
t_en: (It's time to straighten out Penacony's workplace conditions... This hotel's lobby will be my first stop.)

t_ch: （该让宾客们见识下什么叫真正的五星级服务了！）
t_en: (It's time to show the guests what is true five-star service!)

t_ch: 客人您好，请问有什么可以为您效劳的吗？
t_en: Greetings, esteemed guest. How may I assist you today?

t_ch: 你知道我是谁吗？
t_en: Do you know who I am?

t_ch: 呃…没记错的话，您应该是星穹列车的{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}？
t_en: Err... If I'm not wrong, you should be {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME} of the Astral Express, yes?

t_ch: 有什么可以为您效劳的吗，{F#小姐}{M#先生}？
t_en: How can I help you, {F#miss}{M#sir}?

t_ch: 再问一遍，你知道*我*是谁吗？
t_en: I'll ask you again: Do you know who I am?

t_ch: 呃…请问，您是对酒店提供的服务有任何意见吗？
t_en: Err... Do you have any issues with the hotel's services?

t_ch: 如果是这样，还请致电白日梦酒店的服务投诉专线……
t_en: If that is the case, please call The Reverie's dedicated complaint hotline...

t_ch: 我现在的身份可不止这些！
t_en: My status is much more than that now!

t_ch: 呃…您喜欢猜谜游戏，对吗？
t_en: Err... You like riddles, is that right?

t_ch: 您客房里的《进入梦乡》杂志每期都会刊登填字游戏，您得空时可以试着玩玩，打发下时间……
t_en: There are crossword puzzles in every issue of the Diving into Dreamland magazine in your guest room. You can try those out if you wish to kill some time...

t_ch: 那个，要是您没有什么急事的话，我还有些事务要处理……
t_en: Um, if you have nothing else urgent for me, I have to get back to work...

t_ch: 我现在就要当大堂经理。
t_en: I'm going to be the lobby manager right now.

t_ch: 我要整顿匹诺康尼的职场！
t_en: I will straighten out Penacony's workplace!

t_ch: 呃…我没太明白您的意思，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}…介意展开解释一下吗？
t_en: Err... I'm not sure I get what you mean, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}... Would you mind elaborating on that a little?

t_ch: 你将星穹列车即将持股匹诺康尼的事告诉了丹尼斯，并「稍微」夸大了一下事实——比如在「5%」的5后面加了个0……
t_en: You tell Dennis that the Astral Express is about to become a stakeholder of Penacony, and embellish the truth "a teensy-weensy bit" — such as adding a zero behind the five percent...

t_ch: 然后，你表达了自己想要顶替丹尼斯、体验大堂经理工作内容的强烈意愿。
t_en: And then, you express your fervent wish to replace Dennis as the lobby manager and experience his job for yourself.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，姑且不论您所说的消息是否属实…即便您所代表的星穹列车的确持有了集团的股权，这也不代表您可以……
t_en: {F#Madam}{M#Sir}, let's first put aside whether your statement is true... But even if the Astral Express has a stake in the organization and you represent that faction, it doesn't mean that you can...

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬…我看这也未尝不可！
t_en: Ohoho... I think that's not a bad idea at all!

t_ch: 艾、艾弗法先生！您怎么来了？
t_en: M—Mr. Alfalfa! What are you doing here?

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！既然橡木家的家主缺席，我这个「后起之秀」也该多在人前露露面了。
t_en: Ohohoho! Since the Oak Family Head is absent, I have to meet with more people as an "up-and-coming talent."

t_ch: 想不到刚一来就看到了这么一幕——但我觉得无妨！既然这位{F#小姐}{M#先生}是无名客的一员，这也就意味着{F#她}{M#他}将是匹诺康尼集团的长期合作伙伴！
t_en: I never thought I'd see such a scene as soon as I arrive, but I think it's all right! If this {F#lady}{M#gentleman} is one of the Nameless, that means they'll also be one of our longstanding partners with the Penacony Conglomerate!

t_ch: 「体验员工生活」——合作伙伴提出这点小要求，老奥帝我还是可以满足的！嚯嗬嗬嗬！
t_en: Experiencing an employee's life — if a business partner who brings up such a little request, this old man can still grant it! Ohhohoho!

t_ch: 但是，艾弗法先生……
t_en: But, Mr. Alfalfa...

t_ch: 年轻人，别着急！看看你的周围吧，匹诺康尼出了这么大的事，还有几个新客人敢入住酒店？让一位无名客担任大堂经理，这不正是家族迫切需要的正面宣传吗？
t_en: Young one, don't worry! Just look around you — with such a major disaster befalling Penacony, how many new guests would dare stay in our hotel? To have a Nameless become our lobby manager... isn't that the best kind of marketing The Family desires right now?

t_ch: 发现机会、投资机会，多浅显的道理！要我说，鸢尾花家的人缺的就是这点最基本的商业头脑！年轻人，好好看好好学！
t_en: Discover opportunities and invest in them — that's just common sense! Such basic business acumen is what the Iris Family lacks the most, if you ask me! Young one, look and learn!

t_ch: 呃…您教训的是，艾弗法先生。
t_en: Err... Yes, you're right, Mr. Alfalfa.

t_ch: 这态度就对了！嚯嗬嗬嗬…这位尊贵的客人，我该怎么称呼你？
t_en: That's the right attitude! Ohhohoho... Esteemed guest, how may I address you?

t_ch: 叫我「球棒老板」就行。
t_en: Just call me Boss Baseballer.

t_ch: 「球棒老板」！幸会，幸会！
t_en: Boss Baseballer! It's an honor.

t_ch: 叫我「灰总」就好。
t_en: Just call me Boss Gray.

t_ch: 「灰总」！久仰大名，久仰大名！
t_en: Boss Gray! I've heard so much about you!

t_ch: 我是「{NICKNAME}{F#女爵}{M#爵士}」。
t_en: I am {F#Dame}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女爵}{M#爵士}！振聋发聩的名字！
t_en: {F#Dame}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}! A most enlightening name!

t_ch: 我宣布，从现在开始的十个系统时，你就是白日梦酒店唯一的大堂经理！
t_en: I hereby announce that for the next ten system hours, you are The Reverie's one and only Lobby Manager!

t_ch: 需要任何工作上的支持，尽管开口！我会让现实酒店的所有员工都全力配合你的工作，嚯嗬嗬嗬！
t_en: Just speak out if you require any support in your work! I will dispatch all staff working in the hotel in reality to coordinate with you, ohhohoho!

t_ch: 老奥帝又发出一阵招牌的笑声，随后离开了酒店大堂……
t_en: Old Oti lets loose his trademark laughter, and departs the hotel lobby...

t_ch: 别丧气，你又没被炒！
t_en: Don't fret, you haven't been fired!

t_ch: 你在干嘛呢？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: …哈哈哈哈！！真是活久见，谁能想到，我有生之年居然还能碰上这种好事？！
t_en: ...Hahaha! I've certainly lived to see everything now! Who'd have thought I would experience such good luck in my life?

t_ch: 您真是个大善人，我的无名客朋友！工作了这么久，自愿替人上班的我还是头一回见！
t_en: You're a magnanimous person, my Nameless friend! In all my years of work, this is the first time I've seen someone voluntarily wanting to work more!

t_ch: 那么，这里的工作就交给您了——不瞒您说，我在黄金的时刻还有未竟的使命——
t_en: Now then, I'll leave the work here to you. Truth be told, I have other unattended duties in the Golden Hour...

t_ch: 且慢——
t_en: Wait a min...

t_ch: ——工作交接怎么办？！
t_en: ...What about the handover procedure!?

t_ch: …嗯？交接？什么交接？
t_en: ..Eh? Handover? What handover?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，您说笑了！您该不会觉得这份工作有什么专业知识需求或者门槛吧？
t_en: Hahaha, you jest! You thought this job would have professional knowledge requirements or entry barriers?

t_ch: 相信我！以您的硬件条件，只要站在这里、保持笑容就万事俱备了——剩下的只要「顺其自然」就好！没错，顺其自然！
t_en: Trust me! With your overqualified traits, all you have to do is stand here and keep that smile on your face. As for what comes along, just play it by ear! Yep, by ear!

t_ch: 那么，这里就全权交给您了。祝您玩得…呃，不对，祝您上班开心！
t_en: Now, then, I'm bequeathing my entire role to you. Have a great day playing, or rather, working!

t_ch: 丹尼斯以肉眼难以捕捉的速度逃离了白日梦酒店的前台——你难以相信正常人类的股四头肌能够爆发出如此惊人的能量。
t_en: Dennis hastily leaves The Reverie's front desk at a pace faster than the naked eye can see. You can hardly believe a human's appendages can churn out that much explosive strength.

t_ch: （很好…这下整个酒店大堂都归我了。）
t_en: (Very good... Now the entire lobby is mine.)

t_ch: （正式开始工作之前还得先做点准备…嗯…该从哪开始呢？）
t_en: (Better prepare before I officially start work... Hmm, where should I begin?)

t_ch: （自动化服务终端？这可不行…得想办法处理掉。）
t_en: (Automated service terminal? That won't do... Gotta find a way to get rid of it.)

t_ch: 奥帝•艾弗法就这样毫无征兆地突然出现在了你的面前…或许神出鬼没的能力也是「同谐」力量的一种显现？
t_en: Oti Alfalfa suddenly appears before you without any signs... Perhaps his ability to appear out of nowhere is a manifestation of the power of the Harmony?

t_ch: 在你面前的是一台白日梦酒店标配的自动化服务终端。
t_en: Standing before you is a Reverie Hotel's standard-issue automated service terminal.

t_ch: 在客人看来，它集入住、退房、导航服务于一体，是带来便捷的完美工具；但在你眼中，它是夺走无数年轻人工作岗位、彻底掐灭服务行业人性光辉的罪恶机器。
t_en: To a guest, its one-stop-shop services of checking in, checking out, and navigation service make it a perfect tool of convenience. But to you, it is a monstrous machine that has robbed countless job opportunities from aspiring youths, completely rending the service industry of glorious human touch.

t_ch: 更重要的是，它威胁到了你这个大堂经理的地位——它令你的存在形若虚设。
t_en: More importantly, it threatens your place as a lobby manager — it renders your presence and existence redundant.

t_ch: 你绝对不会允许这种事发生。你身为大堂经理的尊严绝不会被一部冰冷、丑陋的机器夺走。
t_en: You will not allow such a thing to happen. Your pride as the lobby manager will not be usurped by a cold, ugly machine.

t_ch: 砸烂它！
t_en: Smash it to smithereens!

t_ch: 「砸烂它！」——球棍如此向你低语到，你把手伸向腰间——
t_en: "Smash it to smithereens!" Your baseball bat whispers to you, and your hand snakes toward your waist...

t_ch: 踢掉它的电源。
t_en: Kick out its power supply.

t_ch: 真的吗？只是拔掉电源就完了？这真的足够「开拓」吗？
t_en: Really? You're just going to remove its power supply? You sure this is "trailblazing" enough?

t_ch: 「砸烂它！」——腰间的球棍如此向你低语到，你把手伸向它——
t_en: "Smash it to smithereens!" Your baseball bat strapped on your waist whispers to you, and you reach for it...

t_ch: ——但转念一想，这台自动化服务终端是酒店的财产——它属于匹诺康尼集团，换句话说，它也属于你这位大股东……
t_en: ...On second thought, this automated service terminal is hotel property. It belongs to the Penacony Conglomerate — in other words, it also belongs to you, a major stakeholder...

t_ch: …好险，你差点就让本能的冲动占了上风。幸好你对钱财的贪恋依旧凌驾于内心难以控制的破坏欲之上——你好像隐约听到腰间的球棍发出了一声叹息。
t_en: ...That was close. You almost let your impulsive instincts get the better of you. Good thing your love for money kept your destructive tendencies in check. You think you hear the baseball bat at your waist sigh in resignation.

t_ch: 「抱歉让你失望了，老朋友，」你在心中默念，「放心吧，以后肯定还有很多机会能让你大显身手……」
t_en: "Sorry to disappoint you, old friend," you lament in your heart. "Don't worry. There'll be plenty of other opportunities to flaunt your power..."

t_ch: 踢掉终端的电源。
t_en: Kick out the terminal's power supply.

t_ch: 「啪」——你一脚踹掉了终端机的电源插头，为那些被夺走美好前程的折纸大学酒店管理专业应届毕业生报了一箭之仇。
t_en: *thud* You dislodge the power supply plug with a swift kick, dealing vengeance for the Paperfold University's hospitality graduates whose bright futures were stolen away.

t_ch: 更重要的是…从现在起，每个需要前台服务的客人都必须来前台找你了。你将每位宾客的生杀大权牢牢掌握在自己手中…你可以用居高临下的语气回应他们的每一次提问。
t_en: More importantly... from now on, all guests who require concierge service will need to come to you. You now hold an iron grip over each guest's life and death... You can respond to every inquiry they have with condescension.

t_ch: 但光是这样还不够…你还需要更多的准备，保证你未来十个系统时的工作体验完美无缺。
t_en: But that's not enough... You still require more preparation that will guarantee a perfect work experience for the next ten system hours.

t_ch: （除了大堂经理之外，前台还有一些其他工作人员……）
t_en: (Besides the lobby manager, there are other staff at the front desk...)

t_ch: （让他们都去享受一天的假期吧！）
t_en: (Let them all enjoy a day off work!)

t_ch: 唔…您好？
t_en: Mmm... hello?

t_ch: 冒昧地问一句，刚才是您把自动化服务终端的电源拔掉了吗？
t_en: May I ask... Did you just unplug the automated service terminal?

t_ch: （骄傲地）没错，是我！
t_en: (Proudly) Yes, that's me!

t_ch: 呃…您还好吗？有需要的话，我可以帮您呼叫护理师……
t_en: Er... Are you okay? If you want, I can call a nurse for you...

t_ch: （冷静地）没错，是我。
t_en: (Calmly) Yes, that's me.

t_ch: 呃…好吧，没关系，您开心就好。一会儿我们再把电源接上就是了。
t_en: Er... Okay, that's fine. As long as you're happy. We'll just plug it back in later.

t_ch: 今天到底怎么回事？怪事一桩接着一桩……
t_en: Did someone put something in the water today!? It's just one weird thing after the next...

t_ch: 还发生了什么怪事？
t_en: What other weird things have been happening?

t_ch: 我专治怪事，说来听听。
t_en: Weird things are right in my wheelhouse. I'm all ears.

t_ch: …奇怪，您不是应该也看到了吗？
t_en: ...Strange. Didn't you see it, too?

t_ch: 丹尼斯经理——他刚才不知道犯了什么病，嘴里喊着假期啊代币啊什么的就冲出去了——简直太奇怪了，我从没见过他那副样子！
t_en: Dennis the manager — who knows what he's dealing with upstairs, but he stormed out screaming things like "vacation" and "tokens"... It was so weird. I've never seen him like that before!

t_ch: 虽然听说梦境里发生了一些状况，但这毕竟还是工作日啊…现在大堂经理带头擅离职守了，留下我们怎么办？
t_en: I heard about something going down in the Dreamscape, but it's a work day so, y'know... Our lobby manager is MIA, too. What are we supposed to do?

t_ch: 现在这里我说了算——
t_en: I'm in charge of things around here now...

t_ch: ——我给你们放一天假！
t_en: ...I'll give you the day off!

t_ch: …欸？您说真的？
t_en: ...Huh? For real?

t_ch: 我收回之前说的话。今天才不是什么奇怪的日子，今天简直就是奇迹日！
t_en: I take it all back, then. Today isn't a weird day — today's a miraculous day!

t_ch: 那么，前台的工作就全都拜托您了…应该没问题吧？既然是老奥帝先生亲自答应的，那出了什么岔子应该也不会追责到我头上？
t_en: Then, I'll leave all the reception work with you... Shouldn't be too difficult, right? Seeing as Old Oti personally assented to this one, I can't get blamed if anything goes wrong, can I?

t_ch: 慢着！我还需要你帮我一个忙。
t_en: Hang on! I still need you to help me with a favor.
...Huh?

t_ch: 需要帮什么忙，您尽管开口——经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}！
t_en: If you need anything, just ask away... {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager!

t_ch: （…这才像样！这才是配得上大股东的办公环境嘛。）
t_en: (...That's more like it! That's more like an office worthy enough for a major shareholder.)

t_ch: （让我想想…好像还缺了点什么。对，得去一趟宴会厅……）
t_en: (Let me see... Something seems to be missing still. Oh yes, I have to go to the banquet hall...)

t_ch: 为了不让前台看上去过于空荡，你找来了一些石头老板的气球作为点缀……
t_en: To liven up the front desk, you add some Boss Stone balloons as decorations...

t_ch: 白日梦酒店的前台。它原本是通向那个奢靡、华贵、醉生梦死的世界的大门，如今在你的努力下变成了…咳，它变成了一种很*新潮*的东西。
t_en: The front desk of The Reverie Hotel. Originally the gateway to that luxurious, opulent, inebriated dream world, it has become thanks to your hard work... *ahem* It's become something very fashionable.

t_ch: 外人可能会将之评价为「一片狼藉」，但你会告诉他们，这应该叫「除旧布新」——当然，你当然知道这个成语是什么意思。
t_en: Outsiders might rate it as "a run-down mess," but you tell them that's just "out with the old, in with the new" — and of course, that's just as you intended it.

t_ch: 别废话了，开始工作吧！
t_en: Enough blabbing already. Get to work!

t_ch: 都听你的，大堂经理{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Whatever you say, Lobby Manager {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 让那群一头雾水的贵宾尝尝你独家的待客之道，老板！
t_en: Give those confused VIPs a taste of your special hospitality, boss!

t_ch: 让客人们再等等…
t_en: Let them wait a little longer...

t_ch: 气氛已经烘托到这儿了，你可不能打退堂鼓……
t_en: The atmosphere is starting to pop off. You can't back down now...

t_ch: 好吧，那就花点时间，再调整一下心态…毕竟接下来你还有十个系统时的工作要做呢。
t_en: Alright. Take a little moment to adjust your mindset... After all, you still have ten system hours of work on the clock.

t_ch: （毕竟经历了那么多大事，前台今天还真是冷清……）
t_en: (After going through all those major events, the front desk seems rather empty today...)

t_ch: 当然，也有可能你过于豪放的装饰风格把客人全都吓——
t_en: Of course, it's also possible that your over-the-top style of decorations has frightened off all the gue—
(...Shut up!)

t_ch: （让我想想…接下来该干什么呢？）
t_en: (Let me think... what should I do next?)

t_ch: 等待下一位宾客到来。
t_en: Wait for the next guest to arrive.

t_ch: 喝一瓶苏乐达解闷。
t_en: Drink a bottle of SoulGlad to get rid of the boredom.

t_ch: 吨…吨吨…吨……
t_en: Glug... glug glug...

t_ch: 一整瓶冰冰凉凉的苏乐达下肚，你现在感觉棒极了。若不是文学造诣实在有限，你现在简直有意立即作诗两首。
t_en: You feel incredible after sinking a whole bottle of cold SoulGlad. If it weren't for your limited literary abilities, you'd probably have written a poem or two about it already.

t_ch: 话说回来，白日梦酒店里有真正意义上的盥洗室吗？反正你没见过。以你消耗苏乐达的速率，你最好提前掌握好一切必要的信息……
t_en: Anyway. Does The Reverie have a real bathroom in here? Haven't seen one. And at the rate you're consuming SoulGlads, you'd best have all the info you need available to you...

t_ch: 擅离职守一会儿。
t_en: MIA for a while.

t_ch: （这会儿好像没什么人…那我先离开一会儿应该也没关系吧？）
t_en: (There doesn't seem to be anyone around here just now... So it shouldn't be a problem if I slip off for a while, right?)

t_ch: 吨…吨…吨…吨……
t_en: *Gulp*... *Gulp*... *Gulp*... *Gulp*...

t_ch: 这一瓶的口感似乎没有那么沁凉清爽了…将饮料瓶净空的瞬间，你感到了一丝后悔，因为你的肠胃发动了代表抗议的微微抽搐。
t_en: This bottle of SoulGlad fails to provide the expected refreshing and revitalizing experience... As you finish the bottle, a slight pang of regret washes over you, as your stomach gives slight shivers of protest.

t_ch: 但相对的，你感觉自己的*诗性*上升到了一个全新的境界。只差一点，还差一点点来自苏乐达刺激…你相信你立刻就能创作出惊世骇俗的绝句了。
t_en: However, in return, you feel your poetic inspiration has ascended to a brand-new level. Just a bit more... Just a bit more of the SoulGlad stimulus... and you will instantly compose a magnificent masterpiece.

t_ch: 浓稠的苏乐达浆液流过你的喉咙，经过消化道，进入胃部…然后，它开始烧灼你的胃壁，剧烈的酸楚感化为一阵波浪翻涌而上，令你发出一阵干呕。
t_en: The thick SoulGlad slurry flows down your throat, traverses your digestive tract, and enters your stomach... And then, it begins to sear your stomach, leading to an intense acid reflux that results in a series of retching.

t_ch: 与此同时，你被迷醉的神经也突破了*诗性的极点*，一串锦心绣腹的词句从你口中连珠而出——
t_en: Despite the discomfort, your intoxicated nerves reach the poetic summit, and a string of exceptionally beautiful words effortlessly pours forth from your lips...

t_ch: 「对饮当歌，人生几何！譬如朝露，去日苦多。」
t_en: "Sing for your life, sipping on soda delight! Embrace the sweet dews, as the day turns to night!"

t_ch: 「慨当以慷，忧思难忘。何以解忧？惟有乐达！」
t_en: "In the haunted days of worries and sorrow, SoulGlad will bring you... bring you... happiness!"

t_ch: …好像不太押韵，但谁在乎呢？对技巧的过度崇拜不过是那些尘俗诗人的拘儒之论罢了，像你这样的鬼才绝不应被那些条条框框束缚……
t_en: ...Even if the last line lacks rhyme, it doesn't matter. Excessive worship of such rhymes is simply the mundane theories of those banal poets. A true ingenious poet like you should never be constrained by such rules...

t_ch: 但再伟大的诗人也是有极限的，或许你应该稍微照顾下五脏六腑发出的抗议，和那些闪闪发亮的苏乐达瓶子保持安全距离……
t_en: However, even the greatest poet have their limits. So, it would be wise to heed the protests from your stomach and a safe distance from those enticing bottles of SoulGlad...

t_ch: 哦，no…你早该学会听取理性的意见的。这下糟了。
t_en: Oh no... you realize that you should have listened to reason long ago, and now you find yourself in trouble.

t_ch: 一团胀气开始在你的下腹部聚集，随后化作汹涌的热浪扶摇直上。
t_en: You feel belching and flatulence in your abdomen, transforming into an intense surge of heat that rises rapidly.

t_ch: 它毫不费力地突破了你人体构造中的每一道关卡，并撬开了你自控力为0的嘴巴；最终，它化作了一滩七色彩虹状的呕吐物，摧毁了你精心布置的工作环境。
t_en: It effortlessly breaches every barrier within your body's construction, forcing open your mouth with no self-control. Eventually, it became a pool of rainbow-shaded vomit that completely ruins your meticulously arranged work environment.

t_ch: 周围本就寥寥无几的客人全都被这骇人的一幕吓跑了…或许这也是一种另类的减轻工作负担的方式。
t_en: The few customers nearby are scared away by this horrifying scene... Perhaps this is another way to alleviate your workload?

t_ch: 这下，你理应学到教训了吧？
t_en: Now, you have learned your lesson, haven't you?

t_ch: 你将这一切怪罪于姬子和瓦尔特•杨——作为列车组的大家长，他们没有教会你「自制力」在充满诱惑的现代社会中是多么重要。
t_en: You find yourself blaming Himeko and Mr. Yang, the "parents" among the Crew, for their failure to emphasize the importance of "self-control" in the face of modern temptations.

t_ch: 不，绝对不行。你听见了吗？绝、对、不、行。
t_en: No, absolutely not. Do you hear that? NO. NEVER.

t_ch: 你颤抖着抽回了那只情不自禁的手。你这一生如履薄冰…绝对不能因为一瓶小小的碳酸饮料导致自己走不到对岸。
t_en: Trembling, you retract the hand that almost betrays your self-discipline. You have been cautious all your life... and now you must not let a bottle of carbonated drink ruin your hard-earned reputation.

t_ch: 再来一瓶苏乐达。
t_en: Have another bottle of SoulGlad.

t_ch: 怎么样？现在有心情继续工作了吗…老板？
t_en: How about it? Still in the mood to keep working... boss?

t_ch: 继续工作。
t_en: Keep working.

t_ch: （不能再拖延了…工作时间到了！）
t_en: (No more delays... Time to work!)

t_ch: 再磨蹭一会儿…
t_en: Drag it out just a little longer...

t_ch: （这会儿好像没什么客人…再随便转转吧。）
t_en: (There don't seem to be any guests right now... Let's take a bit of a walk around then.)

t_ch: 您好，我想办理入住！
t_en: Hello, I'd like to check in!

t_ch: 有预约吗?
t_en: Do you have a booking?

t_ch: 当然！麻烦您查一下，我的名字是罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔。
t_en: Course I do! Please could you look up for me: Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell.

t_ch: 办不了。
t_en: I can't check you in.

t_ch: 呃…但这里不是您管事吗？我看周围也没有别的员工，自动服务终端好像也宕机了……
t_en: Er... but aren't you the supervisor around here? I don't see any other staff around, and the auto service terminals are all dead...

t_ch: …所以只能麻烦您了。请查一下，我的名字叫罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔。
t_en: ...So I'll have to trouble you, then. Please can you look me up? The name's Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}，匹诺康尼集团的股东。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}, and I'm a shareholder of the Penacony Conglomerate.

t_ch: 呃…好的？匹诺康尼集团的股东{F#女士}{M#先生}。现在能帮我办入住了吗？
t_en: Er... okay? {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} Penacony Conglomerate shareholder, could you please check me in now?

t_ch: 麻烦您查一下我的预约记录：我的名字是罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔。
t_en: Please could you look up the name I booked under: Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell.

t_ch: 你没听错，那就是他的名字——
t_en: You heard things right. That's his real name...

t_ch: ——罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔。
t_en: ...Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell.

t_ch: 抱歉…你能再念一遍吗？
t_en: I'm sorry... Could you please repeat that?

t_ch: 当然！罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔。
t_en: Of course! Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell.

t_ch: 凭借无人能敌的内力和天花板级别的职业素养，你压制住了大声嘲笑眼前这位皮皮西人的冲动。显然，他对于自己姓名的滑稽程度毫无自觉。
t_en: With unprecedented levels of restraint and first-rate professionalism, you fight back the urge to laugh out loud at this Pepeshi — who apparently has no idea how silly his name sounds.

t_ch: 好的，罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登…
t_en: Alright, Royroy Bougiebooga Baden...

t_ch: …巴登豪威尔，没错！
t_en: ...Baden-Powell, that's right!

t_ch: 很明显，眼前的皮皮西人对于自己名字的滑稽程度毫无自觉。讥笑别人的姓名是粗鲁的行为——所以你决定只是默默在心里对他进行彻头彻尾的嘲弄…多么仁慈。
t_en: It's clear to you that this Pepeshi has no idea how funny his name sounds. It's rude to just laugh at someone's name — and so you decide to just do so quietly in your head. How kind!

t_ch: 稍等，我来查询一下系统……
t_en: Hang on a minute. Let me check the system...

t_ch: …压根就没有什么「系统」。即使真的有，丹尼斯也没有告诉你该如何操作。
t_en: ...There never was any "system" in the first place. And even if there was, Dennis has never told you how to operate it.

t_ch: 好在匹诺康尼刚刚度过一劫，你*猜测*白日梦酒店现在应该空出了很多房源……
t_en: Fortunately, Penacony has just been through a disaster. You guess that The Reverie Hotel should have a few empty rooms now...

t_ch: 呃…{F#女士}{M#先生}？您不是说要查询一下系统吗？
t_en: Er... {F#Madam}{M#Sir}? Did you just say that you'd like to check the system?

t_ch: 来自宾客的质问如期而至。你手无寸铁，只有灵活的脑回路和一口伶牙俐齿能当作武器——迎难而上吧，战士！
t_en: Questions arise from the guest, as expected. Weaponless, you are armed only with your quick wits and a sharp tongue — take them on, soldier!

t_ch: 好消息：现在有很多空房！
t_en: Good news! There are plenty of vacancies right now!

t_ch: 好消息！你选择的入住时机绝佳，现在正值旅游淡季，白日梦酒店有很多空置的房间。
t_en: Good news! You picked the perfect time to stay, because it's the off season and The Reverie Hotel has plenty of vacancies.

t_ch: 你原本预订的是一间经济客房，现在我们可以免费为你升级为「豪华客房」、「高级客房」或者「舒适客房」！
t_en: You booked an economy room to begin with, but we are now able to upgrade you to a Deluxe Suite, an Executive Suite, or a Comfort Suite — free of charge!

t_ch: 真的吗？！太好了！奥帝叔叔果然靠谱！
t_en: Really!? That's amazing! Uncle Oti's the man!

t_ch: …他刚才是不是提到了*奥帝叔叔*？
t_en: ...Did he just mention Uncle Oti?

t_ch: 突然间，你觉得罗伊罗伊•布奇布嘎•巴登豪威尔这个名字听上去居然没有那么滑稽了。果然，拥有一位富有的亲属是皮皮西人最好的医美。
t_en: All of a sudden, the name Royroy Bougiebooga Baden-Powell doesn't sound so funny to you anymore. Sure enough, having a rich relative is the best cosmetic out there.

t_ch: 坏消息：您的预约取消了。
t_en: Bad news! Your booking has been canceled.

t_ch: 坏消息，巴登豪威尔先生…系统里没有记录，你的预约恐怕被取消了。
t_en: Bad news, Mr. Baden-Powell... There's no record of you in the system. I'm afraid your booking has been canceled.
